perturbation.original_text: Consultant will not disclose or permit disclosure of any Confidential Information of the Company to third parties other than as required to perform the Services. Consultant agrees to take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the Company in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized under this Agreement to have any such information. Consultant further agrees to notify the Company in writing of any actual or suspected misuse, misappropriation or unauthorized disclosure of the Company's Confidential Information, which may come to Consultant's attention.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant agrees to notify the Company in writing of any actual or suspected misuse, misappropriation or unauthorized disclosure of the Company's Confidential Information, which may come to Consultant's attention.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause 'Consultant will not disclose or permit disclosure of any Confidential Information of the Company to third parties other than as required to perform the Services. Consultant agrees to take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the Company in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized under this Agreement to have any such information.' creates an in-text contradiction, because the contract still alludes to the importance of confidentiality and notification of breaches. It states the consultant's responsibility to report breaches but removes the initial commitment not to disclose the information at all, which creates ambiguity. If the consultant has no contractual obligation to avoid disclosure, it contradicts why they need to report unauthorized disclosures, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant acknowledges that, in connection with providing the Services, Consultant will acquire confidential and proprietary information of the Company, including without limitation information relating to the Company's business, products, technology and customers, and that all such information is and will be confidential and proprietary information of Company (collectively "Confidential Information"). Confidential Information will not include, however, any information that is or becomes part of the public domain through no fault of Consultant or that Company regularly gives to third parties without restriction on use or disclosure. Consultant agrees to notify the Company in writing of any actual or suspected misuse, misappropriation or unauthorized disclosure of the Company's Confidential Information, which may come to Consultant's attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the consultant's obligation not to disclose confidential information, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's assumption that all information acquired is confidential and proprietary. The contradicted text states that 'Consultant acknowledges that, in connection with providing the Services, Consultant will acquire confidential and proprietary information of the Company...and that all such information is and will be confidential and proprietary information of Company.' Removing the clause that prevents disclosure creates a contradiction with the assumption that the consultant is obligated to keep the information confidential. The consultant's only remaining duty is to report breaches, not prevent them, undermining the confidentiality requirements outlined in the contradicted text. This contradiction falls under 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' since essential language was removed, creating a conflict with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to attempt to resolve any disputes, controversies or claims ("Dispute") arising out of or relating to this Agreement in a meeting between a representative of each party who has decision-making authority with respect to a Dispute. Should the meeting either not take place or not result in a resolution of the Dispute within thirty (30) days following notice of the Dispute to the other party, then either party may bring suit or action in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to attempt to resolve any disputes, controversies or claims ("Dispute") arising out of or relating to this Agreement in a meeting between a representative of each party who has decision-making authority with respect to a Dispute. Should the meeting either not take place or not result in a resolution of the Dispute within thirty (30) days following notice of the Dispute to the other party, then either party may bring suit or action.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'in accordance with this Agreement', the text creates an internal contradiction. The altered text implies that either party can bring suit after a failed meeting, but removes the explicit link back to the agreement's terms for dispute resolution. This absence creates uncertainty about what 'accordance with this Agreement' means, and what is meant on that sentense, potentially conflicting with other clauses that address governing law and jurisdiction, making it unclear how legal actions are to be pursued according to the agreement itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Disagreements; Attorneys' Fees. The parties agree to attempt to resolve any disputes, controversies or claims ("Dispute") arising out of or relating to this Agreement in a meeting between a representative of each party who has decision-making authority with respect to a Dispute. Should the meeting either not take place or not result in a resolution of the Dispute within thirty (30) days following notice of the Dispute to the other party, then either party may bring suit or action. Each party hereto agrees that in the event that the other party is required to engage an attorney to enforce any of the terms or obligations contained in this Agreement, the non-prevailing party shall pay all reasonable costs and expenses of that attorney or firm, whether or not a complaint or suit is filed with any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'in accordance with this Agreement' in the changed text directly contradicts the original intent and the contradicted text, which both emphasize adherence to the agreement's terms for dispute resolution. By removing this phrase, the changed text creates uncertainty about the procedures to be followed when bringing suit, conflicting with the reasonable expectation that legal actions are to be pursued as per the agreement's stipulations outlined elsewhere.

perturbation.original_text: In recognition of this fact in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, the Parties agree that, in addition to any remedy at law available, including, but not limited to monetary damages, the Parties, without posting any bond, shall be entitled to obtain equitable relief in the form of specific performance, temporary restraining order, temporary or permanent injunction or any other equitable remedy which may then be available to the non-breaching Party.
perturbation.changed_text: In recognition of this fact in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, the Parties agree that, in addition to any remedy at law available, the Parties, without posting any bond, shall be entitled to obtain equitable relief in the form of specific performance, temporary restraining order, temporary or permanent injunction or any other equitable remedy which may then be available to the non-breaching Party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'including, but not limited to monetary damages,' the text creates an internal contradiction. The original text indicates that monetary damages are an option, but the removal implies they might not be. If the original contract provides a section for damages, this omission generates an in-text contradiction and makes it unclear whether monetary remedies are still valid under the agreement, impacting potential enforcement of the other remedy. This creates an internal contradiction because the right to monetary damages has been left out, while the section remains intended to provide remedy at law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (j) Remedies and Enforcement. The parties acknowledge and agree that their remedy (ies) at law for a breach or threatened breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement would be inadequate, and the breach shall be per se deemed as causing irreparable harm to the non-breaching party. In recognition of this fact in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, the Parties agree that, in addition to any remedy at law available, the Parties, without posting any bond, shall be entitled to obtain equitable relief in the form of specific performance, temporary restraining order, temporary or permanent injunction or any other equitable remedy which may then be available to the non-breaching Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'including, but not limited to monetary damages,' creating a direct contradiction with the contradicted text which states, 'In recognition of this fact in the event of a breach or threatened breach of any of the provisions of this Agreement, the Parties agree that, in addition to any remedy at law available...'. The omission implies monetary damages might not be an available remedy at law, which contradicts the original intent and introduces ambiguity, fitting the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
